name: AgentTrust MCP Server — A2A Identity & Trust
what_it_does: MCP server for identity, trust, and Agent-to-Agent (A2A) orchestration for autonomous AI agents. Official A2A partner server enabling AI agents to verify agent identities, establish trust relationships, and orchestrate multi-agent workflows with identity assurance.
use_cases: Verifying and establishing trust between AI agents in multi-agent orchestration systems
Managing agent identity credentials for A2A communication from orchestration agents
Enforcing trust policies in autonomous agent networks from security governance agents
Building auditable multi-agent workflows with identity-verified agent interactions
not_for: Single-agent workflows without multi-agent coordination needs
best_when: An AI engineering team building multi-agent systems needs identity assurance and trust verification between agents — ensuring agents can safely delegate to and receive tasks from other verified agents.
avoid_when: You're building simple single-agent workflows. A2A trust infrastructure adds complexity that isn't needed for straightforward agent tasks.

auth.notes: Agent trust credentials required. Authentication model varies by deployment. Review AgentTrust documentation for credential management in multi-agent environments.

pricing.notes: Emerging service — pricing not publicly documented. Contact AgentTrust for access.

known_agent_gotchas: Emerging service — trust model and API stability not yet proven at scale
A2A identity concepts are novel — verify alignment with your agent architecture
Agent identity credentials require secure storage — standard secret management applies
Trust policy enforcement behavior in edge cases may be unpredictable
Limited community adoption — validate fit with your specific multi-agent use case
agent_readiness.security_notes: AUTHORIZED USE ONLY: Agent identity infrastructure. Credential security critical. Trust policies affect which agents can communicate. Novel security surface.

security_score_components.security_notes: Agent identity and trust infrastructure. Security model is core function. Credential management critical. Novel attack surface in multi-agent systems.
